headline: Corning (GLW) Lands Amazon Fiber Deal As AI Data Center Demand Builds
summary: Corning (NYSE:GLW) has signed a major multiyear fiber optics supply agreement with Amazon. The deal ties Corning directly to U.S. data center expansion and AI infrastructure buildout. Corning is also working with companies such as Nvidia and Meta and ramping manufacturing capacity for optical products. For investors watching Corning, the new Amazon agreement comes alongside a sharp move in the stock. NYSE:GLW last closed at $205.83 and is up 17.3% over the past week, 6.1% over the past...

headline: Chart of the Day: Target’s Risk/Reward Is Improving
summary: While  Target  faces stiff competition from retail giants  Amazon  and  Walmart  the stock offers a compelling combination of value, income, and recovery potential.  Shares showed relative strength on Tuesday, holding up well despite a challenging tape that pressured many consumer and retail names.  Investors are also paid to wait via an attractive dividend yield, while management continues to leverage Target’s nationwide store footprint, strong private-label brands, and omnichannel capabilities.

content.title: Chart of the Day: Target’s Risk/Reward Is Improving
content.summary: While  Target  faces stiff competition from retail giants  Amazon  and  Walmart  the stock offers a compelling combination of value, income, and recovery potential.  Shares showed relative strength on Tuesday, holding up well despite a challenging tape that pressured many consumer and retail names.  Investors are also paid to wait via an attractive dividend yield, while management continues to leverage Target’s nationwide store footprint, strong private-label brands, and omnichannel capabilities.

headline: Amazon pledges additional $13 bn in India AI investment
summary: E-commerce giant Amazon announced on Thursday an additional $13-billion investment by 2030 to expand its artificial intelligence footprint in India, months after declaring plans to invest more than $35 billion."Amazon's cumulative investments in India from 2010-2030 stand at over $88 billion," the company said.

content.title: Amazon pledges additional $13 bn in India AI investment
content.summary: E-commerce giant Amazon announced on Thursday an additional $13-billion investment by 2030 to expand its artificial intelligence footprint in India, months after declaring plans to invest more than $35 billion."Amazon's cumulative investments in India from 2010-2030 stand at over $88 billion," the company said.

thumbnail.caption: Several global corporations have announced large investments in India, which has more than a billion internet users (Kirill KUDRYAVTSEV)

headline: Amazon, Microsoft cloud computing services should fall under EU tech rules, EU regulators say
summary: Amazon and Microsoft's cloud computing services should ‌be designated gatekeepers under EU ‌rules aimed at reining in the power ​of Big Tech, EU antitrust regulators said on Thursday. Amazon Web Services and Microsoft Azure, the two largest ‌cloud providers globally, ⁠should be designated gatekeepers under the Digital Markets Act ⁠which sets out a list of dos and don'ts to ensure ​a level ​playing field, ​the European Commission ‌said. The preliminary findings by the EU competition enforcer came after a seven-month long investigation.

content.title: Amazon, Microsoft cloud computing services should fall under EU tech rules, EU regulators say
content.summary: Amazon and Microsoft's cloud computing services should ‌be designated gatekeepers under EU ‌rules aimed at reining in the power ​of Big Tech, EU antitrust regulators said on Thursday. The preliminary findings by the EU competition enforcer came after a seven-month long investigation.

headline: Amazon to invest additional $13 billion in India cloud, AI
summary: Amazon said on Thursday it will invest an additional $13 billion by ‌2030 in India to expand its AI ‌and cloud infrastructure.  The new investment is in addition to ​its planned $35 billion funding announced last year, taking the e-commerce firm's investment in the country to $48 billion through 2030.  The announcement follows a meeting ‌between Amazon ⁠CEO Andy Jassy and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday in ⁠New Delhi.

content.title: Amazon to invest additional $13 billion in India cloud, AI
content.summary: Amazon said on Thursday it will invest an additional $13 billion by ‌2030 in India to expand its AI ‌and cloud infrastructure.  The new investment is in addition to ​its planned $35 billion funding announced last year, taking the e-commerce firm's investment in the country to $48 billion through 2030.  The announcement follows a meeting ‌between Amazon ⁠CEO Andy Jassy and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday in ⁠New Delhi.

thumbnail.caption: Inauguration of Amazon's new facility in Bengaluru
